name: Visual Effects & Polish
theme: Particles, screen shake, hit flash, death animation, boss defeat slow-mo, iris-wipe transitions, world ambient particles

criteria.description: Camera screen shake system exists with configurable intensity and duration. Camera object exposes a `shake(intensity, duration)` method that offsets rendering.
testSteps: Open browser devtools console
Verify Camera has a `shake` method: typeof Camera.shake === 'function'
Verify Camera has shakeX and shakeY properties
Call Camera.shake(10, 0.5) and observe non-zero shakeX/shakeY values during the next frames
criteria.threshold: Camera.shake(intensity, duration) method exists and produces non-zero shakeX/shakeY offsets that decay to 0 over the given duration

criteria.description: Screen shake is applied to the main render pass. All game rendering (tiles, entities, particles) is offset by Camera.shakeX/shakeY when shake is active.
testSteps: Search the rendering code for references to Camera.shakeX / Camera.shakeY or equivalent shake offset
Verify the offset is applied to the canvas context (e.g., ctx.translate or offset added to Camera.x/Camera.y during rendering)
Confirm the offset does NOT persist after shake ends (resets to 0)
criteria.threshold: The render pass applies shake offsets so all rendered content visibly shifts during screen shake

criteria.description: Screen shake triggers on boss hit (when player damages a boss).
testSteps: Start a boss fight (e.g., stage 1-1 boss Elder Shroomba)
Hit the boss with an attack
Observe screen shake effect on impact
criteria.threshold: Visible screen shake occurs each time the player successfully damages a boss

criteria.description: Screen shake triggers when the player takes damage.
testSteps: Enter any stage with enemies
Allow an enemy to hit the player
Observe screen shake on damage
criteria.threshold: Visible screen shake occurs when the player takes damage (smaller intensity than boss hit)

criteria.description: Player sprite flashes white for a brief moment (2-4 frames) when taking damage, in addition to existing invincibility blinking.
testSteps: Enter any stage and take damage from an enemy or hazard
Observe the player sprite on the exact frame of damage
The sprite should briefly turn fully white before entering the invincibility blink cycle
criteria.threshold: Player sprite renders as white/bright flash on the frame of damage for 2-4 frames

criteria.description: Enemy and boss sprites flash white when hit (already partially implemented via hitFlash). Verify all enemy types across all 4 worlds + citadel flash white on hit.
testSteps: Search enemies.js for hitFlash usage in render functions
Verify every enemy render function and boss render function checks hitFlash and renders white when active
Confirm hitFlash is set to a non-zero value whenever an enemy/boss takes damage
criteria.threshold: All enemy types and all boss types flash white when damaged (hitFlash > 0 triggers white fill)

criteria.description: Forest world (World 1) has drifting leaf ambient particles during gameplay. Leaves drift downward with gentle horizontal sway.
testSteps: Enter any Forest stage (1-1, 1-2, or 1-3)
Observe the foreground for small leaf-shaped or colored particles drifting down
Leaves should drift slowly with a gentle sinusoidal horizontal sway
criteria.threshold: At least 20 visible leaf particles drift across the screen in Forest stages

criteria.description: Desert world (World 2) has drifting sand/dust ambient particles. Small sand grains drift horizontally with slight vertical variation.
testSteps: Enter any Desert stage (2-1, 2-2, or 2-3)
Observe the foreground for small sand-colored particles drifting horizontally
Sand particles should move primarily horizontally (windblown) with slight downward drift
criteria.threshold: At least 20 visible sand/dust particles drift across the screen in Desert stages

criteria.description: Tundra world (World 3) has falling snow ambient particles (already implemented). Verify snow particles are visible and animate properly.
testSteps: Enter any Tundra stage (3-1, 3-2, or 3-3)
Observe snowflake particles falling from the top of the screen
Snow should drift down with slight horizontal wobble
criteria.threshold: Snow particles are clearly visible and animate continuously in all Tundra stages

criteria.description: Volcano world (World 4) has rising ember ambient particles (already implemented). Verify embers are visible and animate properly.
testSteps: Enter any Volcano stage (4-1, 4-2, or 4-3)
Observe glowing ember particles rising from the bottom of the screen
Embers should glow in orange/yellow/red and rise with slight wobble
criteria.threshold: Ember particles are clearly visible and animate continuously in all Volcano stages

criteria.description: World-specific ambient particles only appear in their respective world. Forest leaves don't appear in Desert, etc.
testSteps: Inspect the ambient particle rendering code
Verify each world's particle system checks the current world/stage ID before rendering
Confirm that switching between worlds (via world map) resets/changes the ambient particles
criteria.threshold: Each world's ambient particles are gated by world ID check and do not bleed into other worlds

criteria.description: Player death animation shows the sprite breaking apart into multiple pieces (at least 4) that fall with gravity, rather than just disappearing.
testSteps: Enter any stage and let the player die (lose all HP or fall into hazard)
Observe the death animation
The player sprite should visually break into colored rectangular/geometric pieces
The pieces should fly outward and fall with gravity
criteria.threshold: At least 4 colored pieces (matching player colors) burst outward from the player position and fall with gravity on death

criteria.description: Death particles use player-appropriate colors (blue body, skin tone, dark accents) matching the player sprite palette.
testSteps: Inspect Particles.spawnDeathParticles or equivalent death particle code
Verify the particle colors include player blue (COLORS.playerBlue), darker blue, skin tone, and accent colors
Verify at least 4 distinct colors are used
criteria.threshold: Death particles use at least 3 colors from the player's sprite palette (blue, skin, dark accent)

criteria.description: Boss defeat triggers a slow-motion effect where game speed reduces to approximately 0.3x for about 1 second.
testSteps: Search for slow-motion / time-scale logic in game.js, enemies.js, or states.js
Verify a time scale variable exists (e.g., Game.timeScale or similar) that can be set to ~0.3
Verify boss defeat code sets this time scale when a boss is killed
Verify the time scale returns to 1.0 after ~1 second
criteria.threshold: Boss defeat sets a time scale factor to ~0.3 (range 0.2-0.4) for approximately 1 second (0.7-1.5s) before restoring normal speed

criteria.description: Boss defeat triggers a large particle explosion (significantly more particles than a normal enemy death).
testSteps: Fight and defeat any boss
Observe the defeat effect — a burst of particles should erupt from the boss position
The particle count should be significantly larger than standard enemy death effects
criteria.threshold: Boss defeat spawns at least 20 particles in an explosion pattern from the boss position

criteria.description: Boss defeat particle explosion uses colors appropriate to the boss/world theme.
testSteps: Inspect the boss defeat particle spawning code
Verify the particle colors vary by world (e.g., green for Forest bosses, sand for Desert, blue/white for Tundra, red/orange for Volcano)
Verify the final boss (Architect) has a multi-colored explosion
criteria.threshold: Boss defeat particles use world-appropriate color palettes rather than generic colors

criteria.description: Screen shake accompanies the boss defeat explosion (stronger than normal hit shake).
testSteps: Defeat any boss
Observe that a strong screen shake occurs simultaneously with the particle explosion
The shake should be noticeably stronger than the per-hit shake
criteria.threshold: Boss defeat triggers screen shake with higher intensity than per-hit shake (at least 2x intensity)

criteria.description: Iris-wipe transitions are used for screen transitions (already implemented). The circle closes centered on the player (in gameplay) or screen center (in menus).
testSteps: From world map, enter a stage and observe the iris-wipe open
Complete a stage or die and observe the iris-wipe close
Verify the circle center is at the player position during gameplay transitions
Verify the circle center is at screen center during menu transitions
criteria.threshold: Iris-wipe uses player-centered circle during gameplay and screen-centered during menus

criteria.description: Iris-wipe transition timing is smooth: ~250ms close, brief hold at black, ~250ms open. Total ~500ms.
testSteps: Inspect Transition object in transition.js
Verify duration is set to ~0.25 seconds per phase (close and open)
Verify there is a hold phase between close and open
Verify easing functions are applied (quadratic ease-in for close, ease-out for open)
criteria.threshold: Transition uses ~250ms close + hold + ~250ms open with easing curves

criteria.description: Landing dust particles spawn when the player lands on the ground after falling. Already implemented — verify it works across all worlds.
testSteps: In any stage, jump and land
Observe small dust puffs at the player's feet on landing
Test in at least 2 different worlds to verify it works everywhere
criteria.threshold: Dust particles visibly spawn at the player's feet on every landing in all worlds

criteria.description: Wall slide sparks appear when the player wall-slides (already implemented). Sparks spray from the wall contact point.
testSteps: Find a wall in any stage
Jump toward it and press into the wall to wall-slide
Observe white/yellow spark particles spraying from the contact point
criteria.threshold: Spark particles visibly emit from the wall contact point during wall sliding

criteria.description: Running dust particles appear behind the player while running at full speed.
testSteps: In any stage, hold Shift to run
Observe small dust particles trailing behind the player
Particles should appear periodically (not every frame, but on footstep intervals)
criteria.threshold: Dust particles visibly trail behind the player while running

criteria.description: Game.timeScale property exists and is used by the game loop to scale update speed. When set to a value < 1.0, all game updates slow down proportionally.
testSteps: Inspect game.js for a timeScale property
Verify the fixed-timestep loop uses timeScale to adjust update frequency or speed
Verify timeScale defaults to 1.0
Call Game.timeScale = 0.3 from console and verify gameplay visibly slows
criteria.threshold: Game.timeScale exists, defaults to 1.0, and values < 1.0 produce visible slow-motion in gameplay

criteria.description: Large explosions spawn on breakable block destruction, with block-colored debris particles (already partially implemented). Verify particles spawn and use appropriate colors.
testSteps: Find a breakable block in any stage
Use charge attack to break it
Observe debris particles flying from the broken block
criteria.threshold: At least 6 debris particles spawn from a destroyed breakable block

criteria.description: All visual effects work without causing frame rate drops. The game maintains smooth performance with screen shake, particles, and slow-motion active simultaneously.
testSteps: Play through a boss fight with all effects active (screen shake, particles, hit flash)
Defeat the boss to trigger slow-motion + explosion simultaneously
Verify no noticeable stutter or frame drops during the combined effects
criteria.threshold: Game runs at 50+ FPS during boss defeat sequence with all effects active

criteria.description: No JavaScript console errors are thrown during normal gameplay with all visual effects active.
testSteps: Open browser console, clear it
Play through a full stage with enemies and a boss
Check console for errors related to visual effects (particles, shake, slow-mo, transitions)
criteria.threshold: Zero uncaught JavaScript errors in console during a full stage playthrough

criteria.description: The game loads without errors. index.html loads all script files correctly and Game.init() completes without throwing.
testSteps: Start dev server (python3 -m http.server 8080)
Navigate to http://localhost:8080
Verify the canvas is visible and the title screen renders
Check console for load errors
criteria.threshold: Game loads, canvas is visible, title screen renders, no console errors on load

criteria.description: Screen shake decays smoothly over its duration rather than stopping abruptly. The intensity reduces each frame.
testSteps: Inspect Camera.shake implementation
Verify the shake offset decreases over time (e.g., intensity *= decay factor, or linear reduction)
Verify shake doesn't cut off abruptly but fades to zero
criteria.threshold: Shake intensity decays each frame (multiplicative or linear decay) reaching ~0 by the end of the duration

outOfScope: New gameplay mechanics or level design changes
Bug fixes unrelated to visual effects
Performance optimization beyond maintaining current frame rate
New enemy types or boss mechanics
